title: Build a Readable Color and Type System
summary: Build and verify design color typography in heat-relief brand and readability reconciliation through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain design color typography from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply design color typography without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

steps.title: 01 · Predict the heat-relief brand and readability reconciliation case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim preserves design color typography when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: heat-relief brand and readability reconciliation.

options.text: Heat-relief brand and readability reconciliation case 1: a brand color must be used for text even when it fails contrast.

Heat-relief brand and readability reconciliation case 1: choose type and color roles from reading and state needs, adjust brand usage for contrast, and test real content across zoom, themes, and forced colors.
Heat-relief brand and readability reconciliation case 1: use one faint gray for all secondary text errors and disabled states.

steps.title: 02 · Read the heat-relief brand and readability reconciliation case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 2: Which claim preserves design color typography when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: heat-relief brand and readability reconciliation.
steps.why: Build a Readable Color and Type System makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 2 in heat-relief brand and readability reconciliation.

content.text: Typography and color systems must support comprehension, hierarchy, states, and brand without sacrificing access. Readable line length, line height, font size, resilient system fallbacks, sufficient contrast, and non-color cues matter across zoom and rendering conditions. Brand colors are inputs to refine, not exemptions from contrast. Apply it to heat-relief brand and readability reconciliation.
